name: Beni Suef Governorate (Egypt, 2006-2016)

colors.symbolism: the fertility of the Nile Valley and the agricultural character of the governorate

description: A solid green field defaced in the centre with the white-bordered coat of arms of the governorate, depicting agricultural workers, wheat, and other emblems on a white roundel.
history: In 2006 the Egyptian government established a uniform system of governorate flags: each governorate received a flag bearing its coat of arms on a coloured field. Beni Suef's flag used a green field representing its agricultural character in the Nile Valley. In 2016 a major redesign replaced these governorate-specific flags with a standardised national format, retiring the green-fielded Beni Suef design.

features.symbolism: the agricultural fertility of the Nile Valley in the governorate

features.role: Coat of arms of Beni Suef Governorate
features.symbolism: the governorate's identity, including agricultural workers, wheat, the Nile, and other local symbols
